data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T160837-7cc6c29e/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T160837-7cc6c29e/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T160837-7cc6c29e/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T160837-7cc6c29e/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T160837-7cc6c29e/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-def1c23f">
    <title>feat(work): add ddx work clear-cooldowns bulk operator command</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  Today, clearing a stale cooldown is a per-bead, per-field manual
  operation:
      ddx bead update <id> --unset execute-loop-retry-after \\
                            --unset execute-loop-last-status \\
                            --unset execute-loop-last-detail
  When a SYSTEMIC issue (e.g., a data race in executor.go) blocks
  multiple beads' pushes within minutes, each bead gets its own
  ~24h cooldown. After the systemic cause is fixed, the operator
  must clear each bead one at a time. Observed: 5+ beads required
  manual clear-loops, ~10 minutes of operator time, every time the
  systemic cause is resolved.

  A bulk operator command is needed: scan beads with a matching
  execute-loop-last-status (or all), clear their cooldown fields in
  one pass.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/cmd/bead_cooldown_clear.go (or wherever single-bead clear
    lives) handles one bead at a time.
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go SetExecutionCooldown writes; no
    matching ClearAllCooldowns or ClearCooldownsByStatus.
  - Operators are stuck running --unset --unset --unset loops.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add a new subcommand: ddx work clear-cooldowns [flags]

  Flags:
    --all                 Clear cooldowns on every bead with
                          execute-loop-retry-after set.
    --status <S>          Clear only on beads where
                          execute-loop-last-status==<S>
                          (e.g., push_failed, no_changes_unjustified).
    --dry-run             Print what would be cleared without
                          modifying state.

  Behavior:
    - Scan beads.jsonl (under store lock) for entries with
      execute-loop-retry-after set.
    - Filter by --status if provided.
    - For each match, delete: execute-loop-retry-after,
      execute-loop-last-status, execute-loop-last-detail.
    - Emit a structured "cooldown-cleared" event per bead with
      reason="operator-bulk-clear".
    - Print summary: N cooldowns cleared (or "would clear" for
      --dry-run).

  Implementation:
    - cli/internal/bead/store.go: new method ClearCooldowns(filter
      func(Bead) bool) (int, error) — single-pass batch update.
    - cli/cmd/work_clear_cooldowns.go (new file) — CLI plumbing.
    - cli/cmd/work_clear_cooldowns_test.go: TestClearCooldowns_All
      asserts every cooldown cleared; TestClearCooldowns_ByStatus
      asserts only matching are cleared; TestClearCooldowns_DryRun
      asserts no mutation in dry-run.

NON-SCOPE
  - Auto-invalidation (sister bead handles rev-bound auto-clear).
  - Single-bead clear shortcut (existing path is fine for one-off).
  - GUI / dashboard for cooldown management.

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "fix(try): push_failed must not set cooldown" (R1)
    reduces the FUTURE need for this command, but doesn't eliminate
    it (other cooldowns can still go stale).
  - Sister bead "feat(bead): rev-bound cooldown auto-invalidation"
    (R3) is the long-term elegant fix; this is the operator
    escape valve until that lands.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/cmd/work_clear_cooldowns.go (or equivalent) implements `ddx work clear-cooldowns` with --all, --status, --dry-run flags.
2. cli/internal/bead/store.go contains a ClearCooldowns method (or equivalent) that batch-clears execute-loop-retry-after, execute-loop-last-status, execute-loop-last-detail under the store lock.
3. Each cleared bead has a "cooldown-cleared" event appended with reason="operator-bulk-clear".
4. TestClearCooldowns_All in cli/cmd/work_clear_cooldowns_test.go asserts all cooldowns are cleared.
5. TestClearCooldowns_ByStatus asserts only matching --status beads are cleared.
6. TestClearCooldowns_DryRun asserts no mutation when --dry-run is passed.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, kind:feature, operator-tools</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="1f2c4d33b0f5d4ebfb3b9944e21dcd6b1310b0df" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T160837-7cc6c29e"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All 8 ACs are satisfied:

1. `cli/cmd/work_clear_cooldowns.go` implements `ddx work clear-cooldowns` with `--all`, `--status`, `--dry-run` flags
2. `cli/internal/bead/store.go` has `ClearCooldowns(filter func(Bead) bool) (int, error)` — single-pass batch under store lock clearing the three cooldown fields
3. Each cleared bead gets a `"cooldown-cleared"` event with `Summary: "operator-bulk-clear"` appended inline
4. `TestClearCooldowns_All` — asserts all cooldowns cleared
5. `TestClearCooldowns_ByStatus` — asserts only matching-status beads are cleared
6. `TestClearCooldowns_DryRun` — asserts no mutation in dry-run
7. `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/...` — green
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` — passes
